Mr. VConrad stated that time was of the essence, as the trustee had prusuant to previous order of the Court entered into a reverse mortgage, and the loan is now due since the trustor is deceased and no longer residing in the property. A 90 day notice of default has been served by the lender, and 67 days have already run since its service. If the breach is not cured by a refinancing, the property will be sold in foreclosure, resulting in a loss of $200,000 in equity which would otherwise be available to fund Carol Spinale's Special Needs Trust. The Court stated that an evidentiary hearing would be required. Mr. Berman stated that a subsequent petion was filed with a hearing set for July 12, seeking authorization to refinance the loan and pay off the reverse mortgage.
